top_10.rationale: 'Attention Is All You Need' (Vaswani et al., 2017) — the original transformer paper that introduced the attention mechanism as the sole architectural basis. The primary source for the topic.

The Illustrated Transformer by Jay Alammar — the most widely-cited visual explainer for transformer attention mechanisms; has become the de facto reference tutorial for understanding the architecture.
'Dive into Deep Learning' Chapter 11 on Attention Mechanisms and Transformers — authoritative interactive textbook covering both theory and implementation of transformer attention.

Lil'Log 'Attention? Attention' — Lilian Weng's comprehensive overview of attention mechanisms, covering soft/hard attention, self-attention, and transformers. One of the most cited technical blog posts on the topic.
3Blue1Brown 'Attention in transformers, step-by-step' — Grant Sanderson's visual step-by-step walkthrough of the attention mechanism. Excellent pedagogical coverage of how attention works.
Sebastian Raschka 'Understanding and Coding the Self-Attention Mechanism from Scratch' — practical implementation guide by a machine learning researcher, covering the math and code together.
Comprehensive tutorial and survey 'Attention Mechanism, Transformers, BERT, and GPT' — covers attention, seq2seq, self-attention, encoder-decoder architecture, BERT, GPT. Thorough academic reference.
MachineLearningMastery 'The Transformer Attention Mechanism' — explains scaled dot-product attention and multi-head attention with code examples. Widely-used practical tutorial.
Wikipedia 'Transformer (deep learning)' — encyclopedic reference covering the transformer architecture including attention mechanism, encoder-decoder structure, and modern variants.
IBM 'What is an attention mechanism?' — authoritative explainer from IBM on the attention mechanism's primary function: query, key, value computation for contextual token relationships.
